approach: Retire the two-persona mode-switch (ADR-0022 decision 8). Operator's command-center seat is asserted by invoking the skill — no runtime `cyberfleet mode` probe, no deferral to Pod. In-ship work still reaches Pod by topical routing.

Mode becomes Pod's own precondition: marker absent -> refuse the work, ask the Council to commission, on yes run `cyberfleet init` then carry on with the original request; on no write nothing. Pod never hands off to Operator.

Correct corpus-wide marker drift to `.agents/cyberfleet/ship.json` (the code was always right; `.cyberfleet/`, `.cyberfleet/config.json` and `.agents/cyberlegion/config.json` were all wrong — ADR-0022 decision 8 is the source of the last one).

No code change: `packages/cyberfleet/src/{mode,init}.ts` and `packages/cyberfleet/.agents/spec/**` already correct and untouched. `cyberfleet mode` the command survives; only its caller set shrinks to Pod.
Record as an `## Amendment` on ADR-0022 in place — its naming and query-first decisions stand.
why: leash auto-none: the CR deletes two acceptance scenarios from a @frozen suite, which fires the Clearance hard floor (not self-assertable), and re-opens six more. The owner (unional) holds the user channel live this session and directed the design, so every gate verdict is human-ratified rather than agent-self-asserted.

HITL-ratified live in-session (leash auto-none — Clearance hard floor, not self-assertable). The CR pivoted mid-flight from 'mode is Pod's precondition' to 'mode is deleted' (#225): the owner asked what reads the marker and the answer was nothing — `.agents/cyberfleet/ship.json` held only {version:1}, its sole reader was detectMode, detectMode's sole caller was the `cyberfleet mode` command, whose sole callers were these two personas' mode guards. Closed loop gating no capability; missions/jump/pause/gate never consulted it. Membership already lives in `cyberlegion unit register` -> AgentRecord (what `missions` enumerates, joining ship->CR on worktree.branch). CLEARANCE FLOOR: genuine deletions are the mode-switch pair (Operator activates when there is no ship marker / Operator defers to Pod when it is inside a ship), Pod defers to Operator when it is not in a ship, Operator's commission pair, and Pod's intra-mission fan-out — all moot once the switch is retired. The remaining 'removed' rows are title-keyed retitles that preserved behavior (every mechanic is a cyberlegion call -> offloaded to a CLI; a commissioned ship opens in its own workspace -> every spawned ship). Edit class vs a414b9cd: 17 added / 5 modified / 14 removed. Basis: cold ACED spec-judges over 8 rounds; final judge oracle PASS + builder PASS, architect FAIL on 7 sweep misses, all fixed (incl. plugins/cyberfleet/readme.md, the marketplace front door, which no prior round touched and still had Pod spawning) then grep-gated. pnpm verify 21/21 green. Companion shard: packages/cyberfleet/.agents/spec/ledger/cyberfleet-mode-pod-precondition.440ca1.jsonl — one CR, two touched specs.
